root_text.ud6.8:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā rājagahe viharati veḷuvane kalandakanivāpe.
root_text.ud6.8:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena rājagahe dve pūgā aññatarissā gaṇikāya sārattā honti paṭibaddhacittā;
root_text.ud6.8:1.4: bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ pāṇīhipi upakkamanti, leḍḍūhipi upakkamanti, daṇḍehipi upakkamanti, satthehipi upakkamanti.
root_text.ud6.8:1.5: Te tattha maraṇampi nigacchanti maraṇamattampi dukkhaṁ.
root_text.ud6.8:2.1: Atha kho sambahulā bhikkhū pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya rājagahaṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.8:2.2: Rājagahe piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkantā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu.
root_text.ud6.8:2.3: Ekamantaṁ nisinnā kho te bhikkhū bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.ud6.8:3.1: “Idha, bhante, rājagahe dve pūgā aññatarissā gaṇikāya sārattā paṭibaddhacittā;
root_text.ud6.8:3.2: bhaṇḍanajātā kalahajātā vivādāpannā aññamaññaṁ pāṇīhipi upakkamanti, leḍḍūhipi upakkamanti, daṇḍehipi upakkamanti, satthehipi upakkamanti.
root_text.ud6.8:3.3: Te tattha maraṇampi nigacchanti maraṇamattampi dukkhan”ti.
root_text.ud6.8:4.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:
root_text.ud6.8:5.1: “Yañca pattaṁ yañca pattabbaṁ, ubhayametaṁ rajānukiṇṇaṁ, āturassānusikkhato.
root_text.ud6.8:5.2: Ye ca sikkhāsārā sīlabbataṁ jīvitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ upaṭṭhānasārā, ayameko anto.
root_text.ud6.8:5.5: Iccete ubho antā kaṭasivaḍḍhanā, kaṭasiyo diṭṭhiṁ vaḍḍhenti.
root_text.ud6.8:5.6: Etete ubho ante anabhiññāya olīyanti eke, atidhāvanti eke.
root_text.ud6.8:5.7: Ye ca kho te abhiññāya tatra ca nāhesuṁ, tena ca nāmaññiṁsu, vaṭṭaṁ tesaṁ natthi paññāpanāyā”ti.

translation_text.ud6.8:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the squirrels’ feeding ground.
translation_text.ud6.8:1.3: Now at that time two gangs were both hopelessly in love with a certain courtesan.
translation_text.ud6.8:1.4: Fighting, quarreling, and disputing, they attacked each other with fists, stones, rods, and swords,
translation_text.ud6.8:1.5: resulting in death and deadly pain.
translation_text.ud6.8:2.1: Then several mendicants robed up in the morning and, taking their bowls and robes, entered Rājagaha for alms.
translation_text.ud6.8:2.2: Then, after the meal, when they returned from almsround, they went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what was happening.
translation_text.ud6.8:4.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud6.8:5.1: “What has been attained and what is to be attained are both strewn over with dust for that one training while still sick.
translation_text.ud6.8:5.2: Those for whom the training is the essence, or precepts and observances, chastity, and service as the essence: this is one extreme.
translation_text.ud6.8:5.4: ‘There’s nothing wrong with sensual pleasures’: this is the second extreme.
translation_text.ud6.8:5.5: Thus these two extremes swell the charnel grounds, while the charnel grounds swell wrong view.
translation_text.ud6.8:5.6: Not realizing these two extremes, some get stuck and some overreach.
translation_text.ud6.8:5.7: Those who realize these things, who were not found there, who did not conceive by that, there is no cycle of rebirths to be found.”
